ety: pl. of taʻaiyun; but also used in the sing.
def: s.m. Appointments; appointment, service, duty; a garrison
more: Pop. n. m. 1. Appointments. 2. A garrison. taīnāt k . v. a. To appoint; nominate; enlist; put in possession.
(for تعيينات taﻌyīnāt, plur. of تعيين) s. m. Appointments, appointment, business.
subs: v.t. To appoint, depute, to send on duty; to enlist; to put in possession
v.n. To be appointed, be deputed, be sent on duty.

def: s.f. Appointment, service, duty; taking up the duties of an appointment; the object or business of an appointment, tour of service; a deputation; a detachment of troops; a guard; a garrison; any body of men told off for a special duty.
more: n. f. 1. Appointment; deputation; service. 2. A detachment of troops; a guard, etc. nominated for some special duty.
s. f. The object or business of an appointment, &c. tour of service, attendance, duty.

ety: inf. n. v of عيش 'to live'
def: s.m. Procuring a livelihood by labour and industry, labouring for a living; — living a pleasant life; rejoicing.
more: (inf. v. of عيش) s. m. 1. Rejoicing. 2. Procuring a livelihood by labour and industry.

ety: inf. n. v. of عجب 'to wonder'
def: s.m. Wondering (at); wonder, astonishment, surprise, amazement; admiration
more: n. m. from عجب Wonder (اچمبا). taٴajjub k . v. n. To wonder; admire.
(inf. v. of عجب) s. m. Wondering, admiration, astonishment, wonder, amaze, surprise. تعجب کرنا taʼaﻌjjub karnā, To wonder, admire.
subs: (-par), To wonder (at), be astonished (at); to deem strange or extraordinary; to admire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَجُّب (ta'ajjub), borrowed from Arabic تَعَجُّب (taʕajjub). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi تعجب (t'jb).

ety: inf. n. v of اخر 'to keep or hold back,' &c.
def: s.m. Delay, &c. (see tāḵẖīr); — part. adj. Retarded. postponed; following.
more: (inf. v. of اخرت) 1. s. m. Delay. 2. used participially, Retarded, postponed; following.

ety: inf. n. v of عقب 'to follow'
def: s.m. Searching (after), tracking, pursuing; tracing (intelligence, &c.); — punishment, chastisement.
more: (inf. v. of عقب) s. m. Punishment, chastisement.

ety: inf. n. v of عقل 'to be or become intelligent'
def: s.m. Understanding, perceiving; informing.
more: (inf. v. of عقل) s. m. 1. Understanding, perceiving. 2. Informing.

ety: inf. n. vi of عقب 'to come after'
def: s.m. Following, pursuit; persecution (syn. pīćhā); alternating, alternation, alternate succession.
more: n. m. عقب coming after one. Pursuit; chase ( pīchhā ).
(inf. vi. of عقب) s. m. 1. Pursuit. 2. Persecuting. 3. Alternate succession, following.

def: (imperat. of تعالي taʻālā, q.v.), intj., lit. 'Be exalted'; — taʻāl-taʻal, taʻalaʼllāh, 'God be exalted! Good God! Bravo!

ety: 3rd per. sing. perf. vi of علو 'to be high'
def: adj., lit. 'He is exalted'; or 'be He exalted'; the Exalted, the Most High (God).
more: (from علو) adj. Most high. تعاليٰ الله taāﻌlạ-llāh, The most high God.

ety: inf. n. v of علو 'to be high'
def: s.f. Becoming high or elevated; highness, eminence; exalting ooneself, appearing conspicuous.
more: (inf. v. of علو) s. f. Exalting oneʼs self, appearing conspicuous.

ety: inf. n. v of علق 'to hang or cling (to)'
def: s.m. Being attached (to), being connected (to or with), pertaining (to), dependent (on); attachment; dependance; connexion, relation; relationship; concern, reference, regard; consideration, reflection; commerce, correspondence; means of support, employment, situation, office; property, possession; a manor, &c. = taʻalluqa, q.v.
more: n. m. علق it hung to it. 1. Connexion; dependance; relation; concern. 2. Reference; regard. 3. Office; function; service; employment. Rājā ke hāṅ talluq nahīṅ rahā. taalluq rakhnā . To belong to; pertain to; to be related to; to be concerned or connected with; to have to do with; relate to; refer to; bear upon. 2. To have an interest in. ba-taٴalluq khās . With special reference to.

(inf. v. of علق) s. m. 1. Relationship. 2. Connexion, concern, dependance, property, possession, appertaining. 3. Consideration, reflection. 4. Commerce, correspondence. 5. A manor, lordship. تعلقدار taʼaﻌlluḳ-dār, A landholder. تعلق رکهنا taʼaﻌlluḳ-rakhnā, To appertain, to belong, to depend.
subs: (-se), To be connected (with), to relate (to); to depend (on); to have reference (to), to concern, to pertain (to); — to have an interest (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَلُّق (ta'alluq), borrowed from Arabic تَعَلُّق (taʕalluq), from تَعَلَّقَ (taʕallaqa). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi تعلق (t'lq /⁠ta'alluq⁠/).

ety: for A. تعلقة, taʻalluqat, fem.
def: s.m. Connexion, relationship ( = taʻalluq); possession (of land); fee, manor, lordship, estate; a division of a province, a district
more: n. m. 1. Possession; occupancy. 2. An estate; a manor. 3. A district; a division of a province.
(see تعلق) s. m. 1. Connexion, relationship. 2. Possession of land, fee, manor, lordship. تعلقه دار taʼaﻌlluḳa-dār, Possessor of an estate, a lord of a manor, landlord, feoffee.
s. m. A subdivision of a ضلع ẓilﻌ.
subs: s.m. One who holds a taʻalluqa, a landholder, possessor of an estate, lord of a manor, feoffee
s.f. The tenure, or the status, of a taʻalluq-dār; the estate of a taʻalluq-dār.

def: s.m. pl. (of taʻalluqa), Lordships (of land), manors, estates, &c.

ety: of taʻalluq
def: s.m. Worldly concerns.
more: s. plur. of تعلق q. v. تعلقات دنيوي taʼaﻌlluḳāt-i-dunyavī, Wordly concerns.

ety: inf. n. v of علّ 'to use for the purpose of diverting'
def: s.m. 'Diverting oneself'; whiling away time (with); occupying oneself vainly; making an excuse; procrastination; excuse; objection.
more: (inf. v. of علت) s. m. Procrastination, excuse, objection.

ety: inf. n. v of الم 'to be in pain'
def: s.m. Pain, torment; grief, sorrow; melancholy.
more: (inf. v. of الم) s. m. Melancholy, grief, torment.

ety: inf. n. v of علم 'to know'
def: s.m. Being taught, learning; study; knowledge.
more: (inf. v. of علم) s. m. 1. Learning, knowledge, being taught. 2. Study.

ety: inf. n. of طعم 'to eat'
def: s.m. Eating; — food, victuals
more: n. m. Victuals; food ( bhojan ). Avval taām, bād hū kalām . P. Prov. First eat then speak.
(from طعم Heb. To taste, to eat) s. m. 1. Victuals, food. 2. Eating. طعام بخش t̤aʼāﻌm-baḵẖsh, A large spoon to serve out food with. طعام تلاش t̤aʼāﻌm-talāsh, A sponger.
subs: s.m. A large spoon to serve out food (rice, &c.) with, a ladle
wety: Borrowed from Arabic طَعَام (ṭaʕām).

ety: inf. n. v of عمق 'to be deep'
def: s.m. Diving or going deep (into); penetrating; deepness; penetration; profoundness.
more: (inf. v. of عمق) s. m. 1. Deepness. 2. Penetrating, going deep into.

ety: inf. n. v of امل 'to hope for'
def: s.m. Careful consideration, meditation, reflection, deliberation; deliberate action, pausing, hesitation, cautious procedure; disinclination, scruple
more: n. m. امل hoping. 1. Hesitation; pause; delay; demur. 2. Back wardness; disinclination; shrinking. 3. Irresolution; scruple; qualm; suspense. 4. Question; doubt; uncertainty. 5. Reflection; deliberation; consideration. 6. Caution; care; discretion.
(inf. v. of امل) s. m. Meditation, reflection, consideration, hesitation, purpose. تامل کرنا taʼammul karnā, To collect oneʼs self, consider, reflect, deliberate.
subs: (-meṅ), To consider carefully, to reflect, deliberate; to act or proceed with deliberation (in), to pause, to hesitate, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of اني 'to draw near'
def: s.f. Waiting; delay, procrastination.

ety: inf. n. v of انس 'to be familiar,' &c.
def: s.m. Acquaintance; familiarity; becoming domesticated and tame (as an animal).
(inf. v. of انس) s. m. Acquaintance.

ety: inf. n. v of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. Inquiring with the view to learn, seeking knowledge; acquainting oneself (with), learning; instructing, notifying.

ety: inf. n. v of عرض 'to present or offer itself (a thing)'
def: s.m. Happening, occurring; coming before, presenting oneself (or itself); noticing; opposing, hindering; opposition, hindrance, obstacle, impediment. (A. pl. taʻarruẓāt.)
more: (inf. v. of عرض) s. m. Resisting, preventing, hinderance, obstacle, opposition, impediment.

ety: inf. n. vi of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. Knowing one another; mutual acquaintance; — rule, fashion, custom.
more: (inf. vi. of عرف) s. m. 1. Mutual acquaintance. 2. Rule, fashion, custom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَارُف (ta'āruf), borrowed from Arabic تَعَارُف (taʕāruf), from تَعَارَفَ (taʕārafa).

ety: inf. n. v of عشق 'to love'
def: s.m. Exhibiting or showing love, falling in love (with), being in love; love, affection
more: (inf. v. of عشق) s. m. Being or falling in love, making love.
subs: v.n. To make a show of love or affection

ety: inf. n. v of عصب 'to cling to; to gather round'
def: s.m. Zeal (for a party, or cause); prejudice (for or against); bigotry, religious persecution; superstition.
more: n. m. عصب twisting, aiding one's party. Prejudice; bigotry; religious intolerance.
(inf. v. of عصب) s. m. Prejudice for or against, bigotry, superstition, religious persecution.

ety: inf. n. v of اسف 'to grieve'
def: s.m. Grieving, lamenting, pining, brooding over trouble or affliction; grief, regret, repentance ( = afsos)
subs: (-se), To feel regret (for), to sigh (over); to despair (of).

ety: acc. of taʼassuf
def: adv. With or in grief or regret; remorsefully.

ety: inf. n. v of اثر 'to make a mark,' &c.
def: s.m. Becoming marked or impressed, receiving an impression; impression; penetration.
more: (inf. v. of اثر) s. m. Penetration, passing through, impression, effect.

ety: for A. طاعة, quasi-inf. n. of iv of طوع 'to become submissive,' &c.
def: s.f. Obedience, submission, submissiveness; devotion; obsequiousness.
s.f. pl. (of t̤āʻat, q.v.), Acts of devotion, &c.
more: s. plur. of طاعت q. v.

ety: inf. n. vi of عطو 'to take'
def: s.f. Taking with the hand; giving reciprocal presents; mutual giving or surrender; a silent kind of bargain, the vendor handing over the thing to the buyer, and the latter giving him the price without speaking.

ety: inf. n. v of عطل 'to be destitute (of)'
def: s.m. Being without bracelets or other ornaments (a woman); being or remaining without work or occupation; idleness; being void or vacant (a place, or tent, &c.).
more: (inf. v. of عطل Heb. Was idle, slothful) s. m. 1. Idleness. 2. Having no bracelets or other ornaments.

def: s.m. Assistance; mutual aid; conspiring.
more: (inf. vi. of عون) s. m. Assistance, conspiring.

ety: inf. n. v of عظم 'to be large, great,' &c.
def: s.m. Magnifying oneself, behaving proudly or haughtily; pride, haughtiness, arrogance, insolence; — magnificence, grandeur, majesty.
more: (inf. v. of عظم) s. m. Magnificence, grandeur, pride, arrogance.

ety: inf. n. v of عذر 'to excuse'
def: s.m. Adducing or urging an excuse; excuse, apology.
more: (inf. v. of عذر) s. m. Apology.

def: (inf. v. of عـﮩد) s. m. 1. Agreement, engagement. 2. Rent.
interj. Good God!
interj. Good God! Bravo!
(from طاع) s. f. Devotion, obedience, obsequiousness. (Plur. طاعات).

ety: Pehl. tāp; Z. tāpa; S. ताप, rt. तप्
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; burning, inflaming; pain, affliction, grief; anger, indignation, wrath, rage; light, radiance, lustre, splendour; strength, power, ability, capability; endurance, brooking; — bending, twisting (by heat); bend, twist, contortion; curling, curl; — (as last member of compounds), part. adj. Burning, inflaming, kindling; shining, irradiating; turning, twisting, folding
more: s. f. 1. Heat. (Sans. ताप ). 2. Light, splendour. 3. Power, endurance, brooking. 4. Rage, fury. 5. Twisting, contortion. 6. part. in compos. (of تافتن) Burning, inflaming, illuminating, turning, twisting. تاب تلي tāb-tillī, s. f. An induration of the spleen attended with, or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake. تاب دار tāb-dār, adj. 1. Bright, luminous. 2. Twisted. تاب دينا tāb denā, To twist. تاب کهانا tāb khānā, To be twisted. تاب لانا tāb lānā, To be able to bear.
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever ( = tāp-tillī, q.v.s.v. tāp)
adj. Warm, hot, burning; shining, bright, luminous; twisted, waving, curling
s.m. A passage for light, opening in a wall; lattice, window; skylight
v.t. To inflame; to brighten, polish; to sharpen; to twist
(-ko), To be able to bear, to have patience (with, -par; syn. taḥammul ho-saknā)
adj. Of summer; like summer; pleasant as summer
(-kī), To muster strength, endurance, or courage, &c. (for); to be able to bear
adj. Bright, shining, brilliant, radiant; hot, passionate, choleric

ety: Prk. तेम्व or तेंव; S. तावत्
def: conj. Then, at that time; afterwards; thereupon; therefore, hence
more: adv. Opp. of jab . S. पदा 1. Then; at that time. 2. Afterwards; after this or that. tab-tak ; W. Rus. tab lag, tab tāīṅ, tab hī, toṛī , adv. Till then; until; up to. tab bhī , adj. Still; notwithstanding; nevertheless. tab to , adv. 1. Then; at that time. 2. Whence; wherefore. tab se , adv. Thence; since that time. tab hī , adv. Opp. of jab hī . 1. Immediately; instantly. 2. Consequently; therefore. Kad tum haṅsi-ke kar gahyo, kad ham kahā 'yo nāhīṅ', Ye urhanā tabhīṅ miṭe, jabhīṅ sirhannā bāhīṅ . E. Wom. Song. When did you laughing seize my arm, when said I no? This jar will cease when on my arm your head you throw. tab hī to . For this very reason.
adv. 1. ( तदा ) Then, at that time, so soon. 2. ( ततः ) Afterwards. تب تک tab tak, Till then. تب توڙي ياتئين يالگ tab-toṛī or -taʼīṅ or -lag, adv. Till then, so long.
s. f. 1. Fever: see تپ. 2. Heat.
subs: adv. Even then, then too; still, nevertheless, notwithstanding that
tab-talak, adv. Till then, up to that time; so long, so far
adv. Then indeed, then forsooth, then; whence, wherefore
adv. From that time forward, since that time; thence, thenceforward
adj. Of or belonging to that time
tab-hīṅ, tab-hūṅ, adv. (emphat. of tab), At that very time, just then; there and then, instantly; then indeed; therefore, for that same reason
adv. Only so long, just so long
adv. Then indeed; for that reason then
wety: From Sanskrit त (ta, “this; that”) + एवम् एव (evam eva, “just so”).

def: s.f. = tap, q.v. s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.
wety: From Sanskrit त (ta, “this; that”) + एवम् एव (evam eva, “just so”).

ety: inf. n. of طيب 'to be or become pleasant,' &c.
def: adj. Good, excellent, pleasant, agreeable, delightful, sweet; pure, clean.
more: (Heb. ) adj. Sweet, agreeable, good, pure, excellent.

ety: inf. n. of طبع 'to seal, stamp, impress,' &c.
def: s.f. Stamping, printing; print, impression, edition (of a book, &c.); — nature, innate or natural disposition; genius; natural temper, temperament; idiosyncrasy; quality
more: n. f. طبع stamped. 1. Stamping ( chhāp , 1.). 2. Edition. Taba-i-sānī . Second edition. 3. Nature; temperament (خصلت). taba-āzmāī , n. f. Trial of skill. taba-zād , adj. Original; invented. taba k .; H. chhāpnā , v. a. To print.

(Rab. from طبع Heb. To dip, immerge, mark or coin money) s. f. Nature, genius, temperament, quality, disposition. طبع آزماٴي t̤abﻌ-āzmā-ī, s. f. Trial of genius. طبع زاد t̤abﻌ-zād, Invention, production of genius, produced by the genius.
subs: s.f. Trial of genius or of skill
adj. & s.m. Produced by the genius; original; invented; — a production of genius, an original invention
v.t. To stamp, impress; to print.

def: n. m. تبع following. 1. A servant; a subordinate. 2. A follower; dependant; slave. tābe ; Illit. tābe-dār , adj. 1. Subject; subordinate; obedient. 2. Loyal; dutiful. 3. Yielding; henpecked. tābe-dār banānā, yā karnā , v. a. To compel, or extort obedience or submission; subject; bring under control; subdue.
more: (from تبع) part. act. Dependant, follower, subject. تابعدار tābiﻌ-dār (much used in Hindūstan, though ungrammatical) adj. Dependant, follower. تابعداري tābiﻌ-dār-ī, s. f. Allegiance, obedience, fidelity, dependance. تابع کرنا tābiﻌ karnā, To control, to subject.
part. act. Following, obedient, in the obedience.

def: Illit. n. f. 1. Submission; dependence; allegiance; service; obedience; compliance. 2. Obeisance; homage; fealty; servitorship. tābe-dārīk , v. n. 1. To serve; obey; submit to. 2. To follow; attend upon; to be under. Phr. To do one's bidding.
s.m. See تابه tāba.
more: s. m. A fryingpan. See تابه.

ety: rel. n. from tāb, q.v.
def: s.m. A frying-pan; a skillet.
more: s. m. A fryingpan, a skillet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تابه (tāba, “frying pan”), related to تاب (tāb, “heat”); cognate with Urdu توا (tavā).

ety: inf. n. v of بدل 'to change'
def: s.m. The becoming changed or altered; change, &c. = the next, q.v.
more: s.m. (inf. v. of بدل) Change, alteration, difference, transposition. تبديل کرنا tabdīl karnā, To change, to alter. تبديل مکان tabdīl i-makān, Migration.

def: s.f. pl. (of t̤abīʻat, q.v.), Natures, temperaments, constitutions, dispositions, &c.

ety: Pehl. tavāh; Z. tapāh; S. tapasya
def: adj. Ruined, undone, spoiled, abject, wretched; bad, wicked, depraved
more: adj. Z. tavāh, tapāh . Undone. See بوباد tabāh-hāl , adv. In a deplorable condition; very badly off.
adj. Bad, wicked, depraved, abject, spoiled, ruined, wretched. تباه حال tabāh-ḥāl, adj. Ruined.
subs: adj. & adv. Ruined, &c.; — in wretched plight or condition, miserably poor or low.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَاه (tabāh).

def: adj. contrac. of tabāh, q.v.
more: contract. of تباه tabāh, which see.

ety: inf. n. v of بحر 'to enlarge,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating into the depths of science; being profoundly learned; — rolling in wealth.
more: (inf. v. of بحر) s. m. Profoundness in learning.

def: s.f. Ruin, wreck, destruction, perdition; wretchedness, misery; wickedness, depravity
more: s. f. Wickedness, depravity, ruin, wreck, perdition. تباهي کهاني tabāhī khānī, To be afflicted. تباهي زده tabāhī-zada, adj. Ruined, afflicted, distressed.
subs: v.n. To experience misery; to be afflicted.

def: n. f. Destruction; wreck; ruin.
adv. (prov) = tab-hī, q.v.s.v. tab.
n. m. بان it became separated. 1. Contradiction; opposition; consistency. 2. ( Arith .) Incommensurability.

ety: v.n. fr. طبق 'to stick or cleave to'; or from iv اطبق 'to cover'
def: s.m. A cover, lid; a dish, plate; a tray (esp. a round one = t̤abāq); a disc, an orb; a sphere, world; vault (of heaven); a leaf; gold-leaf, tin-foil, &c.; surface of the earth; layer, stratum; crust; coat (of an onion, &c.); one thing following another, or consecutive; story (of a house); a platform; class, order, degree.
more: n. m. طبق covered. 1. A disc. 2. Region; kingdom; world. Ek rakābī meṅ hameṅ chaudah tabaq raushan hue . Nazīr. A bellyful off one dish ate, seven worlds above and seven below I saw elate. 3. Crust; layer ( parat ). 4. Gold leaf; tinfoil, etc. tabaq chhoṛnā , v. n. Mah. Wom. To present a votive offering in a dish. Paryoṅ kā tabaq chhoṛūṅgī dīvānī na ho jāūṅ, Kuchh khoṭ hai jo khuāb meṅ daryā nazar āyā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. To fairies I will offering make lest I grow mad, All is not right, a river seen in a dream I had.

s. m. (dakh. s. f.) 1. A cover. 2. A plate, a dish, a leaf, a disk. 3. Gold leaf, tinfoil, &c. 4. One thing following another or consecutive; the story of a house, a layer or stratum, &c. class, order, degree.
s. A large brass dish, a tray.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَبَق (tabaq), borrowed from Arabic طَبَق (ṭabaq), from طَابَق (ṭābaq), borrowed from Middle Persian tʾp̄k', from Proto-Iranian *tāpaka-.

ety: prob. for A. t̤ibāq, pl. of t̤abaq, q.v.
def: s.m. A large dish or plate, a tray; a vessel for kneading dough in; a basin, bowl (syn. t̤asht).
more: n. m. 1. A large dish or tray. 2. ( Slang ) The crown of the head. Aisā khoprī par dīyā ki tabāq khul gayā. tabāq sā muṅh . ( Slang ) A flat face. tabāqī-kuttā, Slang , n. m. One who sponges.
(from طبق) s. m. A vessel for kneading dough in.

ety: dim. of t̤abaq
def: s.m. A small plate; a saucer; a small tray, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of بختر 'to walk with an elegant gait'
def: s.m. Walking with a proud and self-conceited gait, strutting; inclining from side to side in walking.
more: (inf. ii. of بختر) s. m. Strutting, walking with a haughty gait or a rolling motion.

def: A sponger.

ety: rel. n. fr. t̤abāq
def: adj. Of or relating to the plate or platter

ety: t̤abaq, q.v. + ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) A small box; one of the bells or rattles that are let into the disc of a tambourine or timbrel.
s.m. One who plays on the t̤abla; a drummer.
more: n. m. One who plays on the tablā , q. v.

def: s.m. One who plays on the t̤abla; a drummer.

ety: imperf. part. of tāftan; see tāb
def: adj. Hot, burning; light, luminous, shining, radiant, brilliant, resplendent, glittering.
more: (from تافتن) adj. Resplendent, splendid, glittering, refulgent, burning.

def: s.m. = tamanćā, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow
s.m. = تبنچه tabanća, and تماچه t̤amāća, qq.v. s.m. = tamanćā, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow
s.m. A slap, blow ( = تماچه tamāća, q.v. s.m. Slap, box, blow

ety: i.q. ḍābar, q.v.
def: s.m. A small lake, a pond, tank, pool (cf. ṭabbar; ḍabrā); — a hut, cottage (cf. ṭāpā, ṭāprā); family, household (cf. tabār).

n. m. 1. A pond; a small lake. 2. A hut; cottage. 3. A family; household; relations. Kelā ek kumāvan-hārā, siglā ṭābar khāve sai, Khāvan-hār na koī niputtā oh kā kār baṭāve sai . Rus. song. Mahmūd. Just one bread-winner sole, the eaters are the household whole, 'Mong all the wretched eaters there's not one would share his toil. 4. (Māṛ). A boy.

ety: 6th conjn. of برك 'to become steady or firm,' &c.

more: v. Be blessed, bless. تبارک الله tabārak-allāh, God be blessed.

ety: inf. n. v of برك 'to kneel and lie down'
def: s.m. Looking for a blessing (by means of); auguring good (from); blessing, benediction; congratulation; a portion of presents (or what is left of food presented to great men, &c.) given to their dependants; sacred relics; — commutation for an offering incumbent on a religious mendicant holding an endowment.
more: n. m. 1. Sacred relics. See پرشاد (2). 2. Commutation for an offering incumbent upon a religious mendicant holding some endowment. Mullā kī ḍāṛhī tabarruk meṅ gaī . Prov. The Mulla's beard goes in relics. ☞ It is related of a Mulla who was distributing sacred tokens among his disciples that a wag, taking a fancy to his beard, plucked a hair which he desired to keep as a sacred relic. Thereupon another and another did the same till at last, in spite of the poor man's protestations, he was left without a beard.

(inf. v. of برکت Heb. He blessed) s. m. 1. Benediction, congratulation. 2. A portion of presents (or what is left of food presented to great men, &c.) given to their dependants; sacred relicks. (Plur. تبرکات).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَرُّک (tabarruk), borrowed from Arabic تَبَرُّك (tabarruk), from تَبَرَّكَ (tabarraka). First attested in 1682 as Middle Hindi تبرک (tbrk /⁠tabarruk⁠/).

ety: acc. of tabarruk

more: s. Finest white sugar very hard or candied.

more: n. f. Bamboo sugar or manna; a siliceous deposit in the joint of the bamboo.

def: s.f. Changing, altering; change, alteration, mutation, modification, substitution; transformation; transposition; — tabdīl karnā (-kī), To change, alter; to transfer, &c. (see badalnā)
more: n. f. بدل changing. Mutation; change; alteration; modification; transposition; transformation. tabdīl-i-āb o havā , n. f. Change of air or climate. tabdīl-i-harkat , G. G. Change of motion. tabdīl karnā . See badalnā , v. a. (2, 4—6). tabdīl-i-nā-jāiz karnā . To tamper (with a document); forge; falsify. tabdīl honā , v. n. To be changed. See badalnā , v. n. tabdīl-i-haiyat karnā ; Ped. for H. bhes badalnā , v. a. To disgufse; personate; to pretend to be.

Saying. tabīat ulajhnā , v. n. To be entangled, involved, puzzled. tabiat ānā , v. n. To be attached to, or in love with. tabiat bahāl h. , v. n. To recover from illness. tabiat par zor ḍālnā , v. a. To exercise one's skill; try one's best. tabiat bigaṛnā , v. n. 1. To feel sick or nausea. 2. To be excited, or randy. tabiat bhar jānā , v. n. To cloy; to be satiated. tabiat bahalnā , v. n. To be amused or diverted. tabiat bīmār, nā sāz, yā alīl honā , v. n. To be ill. tabiat-dār , Ped. adj. See zahīn . Clever; sharp; inventive. tabiat laṛānā , v. a. To tackle a difficulty. tabiat laṛnā , v. n. To tackle (a problem). tabiat lagnā , v. n. 1. To take interest or pleasure in. 2. To be attached to; to take one's fancy. ta…
(from طبع) s. f. Genius, nature, disposition, temperament, constitution, essence, property. طبيعت شناس t̤abīʼaﻌt-shinās, s. m. A physician.

ety: v.n. fr. طبّ; see t̤ibb
def: s.m. A physician (usually applied, in India, to a Moḥammadan practitioner; the term ḍākṭar being applied to those, Europeans or natives, who treat according to the European school of medicine).
more: n. m. A Mah. physician. 1. Mashhūr tabīb aur baid hue, yā paṛh-kar ilm tibābat kā, Dālān kītāboṅ se rokā aur nuskhoṅ se sandūq bharā, tibābat kā, Jab maut maraz ne ān līyā sab bhūle nabz aur qārūrā, Go nuskhe lākh mujarrab the par kām na āyā ek nuskhā . Nazīr. O leech renowned! O learn'd in medicine lore! Your hall is choked with books, your endless store Of recipes bursts your cabinets—anon Come death or sickness, all your art is gone, Your skill to test the pulse, the urine fails, Of all your tried prescriptions none avails. 2. Pesh-i-tabīb marau, pesh-i-kār-āzmūdah birau . Prov. Fly from the doctor, go to the experienced. tabīb-i-hāziq , n. m. An experienced physician; a good doctor.
(verbal adj. from طبّ) s. m. A physician. (Plur. Arab. اطبّا Pers. طبيبان).

more: (inf. v. of ديانت) s. Religiousness, constancy in religion.

def: prop. تڙاك taṛāk, q.v.

def: s.m. Overtaking, visiting (with punishment, &c.); punishment, chastisement, infliction of a fine, &c.; repairing, amending; reparation, remedy; expedient, measure; management; preparation; provision; precaution; the instruments and means used to ward off harm, or to procure justice (as writings, lawyers, witnesses, &c.)
more: n. m. درک overtaking. visiting with. 1. Chastisement; infliction of fines or punishment; punishment. 2. Management; provision; arrangements. tadāruk saṅgīṅ . Severe measures.

(inf. vi. of درک) s. m. 1. Reparation, remedy, an expedient, precaution, provision, preparation (particularly, to obtain justice). 2. The instruments and means used to procure justice (as, writings, lawyers, witnesses, &c.). 3. Chastisement, retaliation. تدارک کرنا tadāruk karnā, To provide against, to make preparation, to take precaution, to redress, oppose, to chastise.
subs: (-kā), To inflict punishment, &c. (on), to chastise; to make reparation (for), to redress; to provide (against), make preparation (for), to guard (against), to take precautions; to oppose.

more: (Pers. تذرو) s. m. A pheasant (to whose gait that of a mistress is compared).

= H تڐايا तडाया taḍāyā, See taṛāwā and taṛāyā.

def: s.f. Treating oneself medically; administering medicine, applying a remedy, curing, healing.
more: (inf. vi. of دوا) s. f. Administering medicine, cure, healing.

ety: inf. n. vi of دول 'to come round in turn'
def: s.m. Taking, or doing, by turns; handing from one to another; tradition.
more: (inf. vi. of دول) s. m. Tradition. ( lit. Passing from one to another).

def: = H تڐاوا तडावा taḍāwā, See taṛāwā and taṛāyā.

more: n. f. دبر forecasting. See اُپايے Conduct; management; prudence; economy; address; skill. tadbīr-i-bāham . Private arrangement. tadbīr-i-khām , n. f. Shallow device. tadbīr-i-saltanat, yā mam'lukat . Politics; government; administration; policy. tadbīr se , adv. Skilfully; economically. tadbīr fāsid . Artifice; plot; machination. tadbīr-i-fāsid se , adv. Fraudulently. tadbīr karnā , v. a. See H. upāë k . husn-i-tadbīr . Good policy; good management. munāsib tadbīr . A proper remedy or measure.

(inf. ii. of دبر) s. f. 1. Deliberation, counsel, contrivance, expedient, opinion, advice. 2. Government, regulation, arrangement, order, management, policy, prudence. (Plur. تدبيرات tadbīrāt ). تدبيرسلطنت tadbīr-i-salt̤anat, s. f. Politicks. تدبيرغذا tadbīr-i-g̠ẖiẕā, s. f. Regimen of diet.
subs: s.f. A vicious plan, a plot, artifice, machination
s.f. Regulation or regimen of diet
(-kī), To deliberate (about); to arrange (for); to form a plan (for or against); to provide (for or against); to dispose (of), &c. (syn. upāy karnā, q.v.)

more: (inf. ii. of دفن) s. f. Burial, interment, funeral.

def: s.f. Anointing, oiling.
(inf. ii. of دهن) s. Anointing.

def: s.f. Discipline, correction, amendment, admonition, chastisement; erudition
more: n. f. ادب he became well-disciplined. Admonition; correction; chastisement. tālīb-khānā , n. m. A reformatory.
(inf. ii. of ادب) s. f. Correction, chastisement, amendment, erudition, discipline. تاديب کرنا tādīb karnā, To instruct, correct, chastise.
subs: (-kī), To instruct, to teach good manners or politeness; to discipline, correct, admonish, chastise.

ety: for A. تعدية taʻdiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of عدو

def: s.f. Gradation, scale
more: n. f. درج went step by step. Graduation; scale. ba-tadrīj , adv. By degrees; gradually (āhista) .
(inf. ii. of درج) s. f. Gradation, scale. بتدريج ba-tadrīj, By degrees.

def: adj. Like that, such like; like him or it, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of درس 'to read'
def: s.f. Instruction by means of reading; lecturing.
more: (inf. ii. of درس) s. f. Giving lectures, instruction by means of reading.

more: (orig. Nocturnal rounds or watchmen, from طوف To go round) s. m. People, tribe, a troop, a band (particularly, of musicians and dancing women); suit, equipage.

def: s. m. (dakh.) Fatherʼs elder brotherʼs son.
s. f. Fatherʼs elder brotherʼs daughter.

def: s.m.f. Vapour, exhalation, steam, mist.

ety: inf. n. ii of فہم 'to understand'
def: s.m. Understanding; conceiving, apprehending.
(inf. v. of فـﮩم) s. m. Understanding, conceiving.

ety: inf. n. v of فحص 'to search (for)'
def: s.m. Search, investigation, examination, scrutiny, inquiry, disquisition.
more: (inf. v. of فحص) s. m. Investigation, inquiry, search, disquisition.

ety: inf. n. v of فجر 'to open (a channel for water, &c.)'
def: s.m. Bursting forth or out; suppuration.
more: (inf. v. of فجر) s. m. Suppuration, bursting forth.

ety: inf. n. v of فخر 'to glory or boast'

ety: inf. n. vi of فخر 'to glory or boast'
def: s.m. Glorying, boasting; boast, vaunt.
more: (inf. vi. of فخر) s. m. Boasting, vaunt.

ety: inf. n. v of فقد 'to be lost'
def: s.m. Searching for anything lost; inquiring diligently, wishing to know; (fig.) seeking to gain the heart (of), sympathy, kindness, commiseration (syn. dil-joʼī, mihrbānī).
more: (inf. v. of فقد Heb. Was wanting) s. m. 1. Searching for a thing lost. 2. Kindness.

ety: inf. n. v of فکر 'to think (upon)'
def: s.m. Thinking (upon), considering; reflection, meditation, cogitation; anxiety.
more: (inf. v. of فکر) s. m. 1. Reflection, meditation, cogitation, considering. 2. Anxiety.

ety: inf. n. v of فنّ; see fan
def: s.m. Diversion, relaxation, pastime, amusement, fun.
more: (inf. v. of فنّ) s. m. Amusement, pastime, diversion, relaxation.

def: s.m. pl. (of tafriqa), Separations, divisions; intervals; instalments.

ety: inf. n. v of فرح 'to be glad'
def: s.m. Ease; leisure; refreshment. (The word is not classical Arabic.)
more: (inf. v. of فرح) s. m. Leisure, ease, refreshment.

ety: inf. n. v of فرج 'to make an opening or break (in)'

more: (inf. v. of فرج) s. m. Recreation, amusement, relaxation of mind.
subs: s.f. Place of recreation (as a garden, or a theatre, &c.).

ety: inf. n. v of فرس 'to be skilled' (orig. 'in horsemanship,' and thence, in anything)

more: (inf. v. of فراست) s. m. 1. Intelligence, understanding, discernment. 2. Judging from physiognomy.

ety: inf. n. vi of فرق 'to make a difference (between)'
def: s.m. Separation, division.
more: (inf. vi. of فرق) s. m. Division, separation.

(inf. vi. of فال) s. m. See تفول.

more: n. m. A. فوت distance. See انتر 2 to 4. Distance; remoteness.
(inf. vi. of فوت) 1. s. m. Distance. 2. Difference, distinction, disparity. 3. adj. Distant, separate, away, absent. تفاوت بولنا tafāwut bolnā, To prevaricate.
subs: v.n. To make incongruous or discordant statements, to prevaricate
(-ko), To put, set, or cast apart or aside.

ety: inf. n. v of فوق 'to be superior (to)'
def: s.m. Exalting oneself (above others), pretending to a superiority (over others); superiority, ascendancy; — doing (anything) leisurely or by degrees.
more: (inf. v. of فوق) s. m. 1. Pretending to a superiority over others. 2. Doing any thing by degrees.

ety: inf. n. v of فأل; but formed from fāl, q.v.
def: s.m. Auguring good (by or from), regarding as a good omen; taking an omen (from); presaging happily; augury
more: (inf. v. of فال) s. m. Taking an omen (especially a good one from the word of another).

ety: inf. n. v of فضل 'to exceed, to excel'
def: s.m. Excess; excelling; deserving; conferring a benefit (on); beneficence, kindness, favour.
more: (inf. v. of فضل) s. m. 1. Beneficence, kindness, deserving. 2. Excelling, excess. (Plur. تفضلات).

def: part. adj. Full to overflowing (a vessel, river, &c.), running over; full of wine, intoxicated.
more: (part. act. of طفح) adj. Overflowing, full of drink.

ety: inf. n. ii of فہم
def: s.f. Making (one) understand or know; teaching instructing, informing.
(inf. ii. of فـﮩم) s. f. Teaching, instructing, informing.

def: s.f. Effacing, obliteration; amending, adjusting.
s.m. Pride; affected magnificence.
s. (dakh.) Blow, slap, box on the ear ( Harris ).

ety: inf. n. ii of فرد 'to be or become single or alone'
def: s.f. Separation from others, retirement from the world (esp. for the sake of devotion); the being unique in science, &c.
more: (inf. ii. of فرد) s. f. Separation from others, retirement from the world (especially, for the sake of devotion), the being unique in science, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرح 'to be glad'
def: s.f. Rejoicing, gladdening, exhilarating; gratification; diversion, amusement, recreation; fun, jest
more: n. f. A. فرح expanding. 1. Rejoicing; diversion; exhilaration. 2. Recreation; taking the air, or a holiday. 3. Jest ( mazāq ). tafrīh-i-tabā , n. m. See تفريح (1.) Cheerfulness; hilarity. tafrīh k . v. n. 1. To refresh or divert oneself. 2. To laugh and jest. tafrī'han , adv. 1. For the sake of recreation. 2. In jest; humourously ( haṅsī se ).
(inf. ii. of فرح) s. f. Rejoicing, exhilarating, amusement, gratification. تفريح طبع tafrīḥ-i-t̤abﻌ, Ease of mind, cheerfulness, hilarity.
subs: s.f. Diversion of mind; ease of mind; cheerfulness, hilarity
v.n. To divert or amuse oneself, to take recreation; to make fun, to be cheerful, to laugh and joke.

ety: acc. of tafrīḥ
def: adv. By way of diversion or amusement, for the sake of recreation; in fun, in jest.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرق; see tafriqa
def: s.f. Separating, dividing; distinguishing, discriminating; separation, division; analysis; distribution, partition, participation; distinction, difference, variety; classification; class, department; (in Arith.) subtraction; — jealousy, suspicion; misunderstanding
more: n. f. 1. Partition; severalty division; division of land ( baṭwāṛā ). 2. Classification; class; department. 3. Distinction; difference; variety (انتر). 4. ( Math .) Subtraction. tafrīq k. , v. n. 1. To distinguish; separate; specify; define; classify. 2. ( In law ) To sever; partition; allot. 3. To subtract; deduct; take away.
(inf. ii. of فرق) s. f. 1. Separation, division, analysis. 2. Partition, participation. 3. Jealousy, misunderstanding. 4. Department. تفريق کرنا tafrīḳ karnā, To analyze.
subs: (-kī), To separate; to analyse; to divide, partition, allot; to distinguish, discriminate, specify, define; to classify; to subtract
s.m. A deed specifying the shares allotted to different parties.

ety: for A. تفرقة, tafriqat, fem., inf. n. ii of فرق 'to make a difference (between)'

more: (inf. ii. of فرق) s. m. 1. Separation, dispersion, distinction. 2. Distress. 3. Divorce. (Plur. تفاريق). تفرقه کرنا tafriḳa karnā, To separate, to distinguish.
subs: (-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to set at variance, to separate
(-kā), To separate, divide; to distinguish, discriminate; to scatter, disperse.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرس 'to break the neck (of an animal)'
def: s.f. Breaking or tearing in pieces, rending.
more: (inf. ii. of فرس Heb. He broke) s. f. Rending, breaking or tearing in pieces.

ety: perf. part. of tafs-īdan; rt. Zend tafś, for tap = S. तप्
def: part. adj. Warmed, heated, burning; burnt; cracked or chapped (lips).
more: part. pass. (of تفسيدن) 1. Burning, heated, warmed. 2. Cracked (lips).

ety: inf. n. ii of فصل 'to separate or divide'
def: s.f. Analysis, separation, division; explaining distinctly or in detail; explanation; particulars, details
more: n. m. A. فصل cutting, defining. 1. Details; particulars; minuteparticulars; specification; exposition; explanation. tafsīl-i-hāl , Particulars of a case. ba-tafsīl-i-zail, muffasilah zail , In form and manner following; as follows. tafsīl k., yā likhnā , v. n. To exhibit in detail; enumerate; particularize; explain at full length; specify. tafsīl-vār, tafsīlan , adv. In detail ( bil-tafsīl ); circumstantially; particularly. tafsīl yeh hai . On this wise; to wit. pūrī tafsīl . Full particulars or details.
(inf. ii. of فصل) s. f. Explanation, analysis, separation, division, detail. تفصيل کرنا tafṣīl karnā, To detail. تفصيلوار tafṣīl-wār, adv. Distinctly, in detail.
subs: (-kī), tafṣīl likhnā (-kī), To give the detail (of), exhibit in detail, to detail; to specify, particularize, explain in full or minutely
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَفْصِیل (tafsīl), from Arabic تَفْصِيل (tafṣīl). First attested in c. 1645 as Middle Hindi تفصیل (tfṣyl).

ety: acc. of tafṣīl
def: adv. In detail, &c. = tafṣīl-wār, q.v.s.v. tafṣīl.

ety: inf. n. ii of فسر 'to disclose; to explain'
def: s.f. Explaining, expounding, interpreting; explanation, exposition, interpretation (esp. of the Qorʼān); paraphrase; commentary.
more: n. f. A. فسر he analyzed. Explanation; commentary ( ṭīkā ); an interpretation, especially of the Qurān .
(inf. ii. of فسر Heb. He interpreted) s. f. Explanation, commentary, paraphrase, an interpretation of the ḳurʼān.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَفْسِير (tafsīr).

s.m. 1. A kind of silk, taffeta. 2. A colour in pigeons and horses, glossy cream colour.

more: (for تافته part. pass. of تافتن) 1. adj. Hot, burning, melted. (Plur. تفتگان). 2. s. m. Quick lime.
subs: adj. Displeased or grieved at heart.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Heated state; heat, warmth; ardour, fervour; trouble, anguish, teen.
more: (in compos.) s. f. Heat, ardour, trouble (of mind, &c.).

ety: inf. n. ii of فتش 'to examine,' &c.
def: s.f. Examination, scrutiny, investigation; diligent inquiry or search, research
more: n. f. from فتش examining, searching. Search; scrutiny; diligent investigation; examination.
(inf. ii. of فتش) s. f. Examination, inquiry, search, investigation, research.
subs: (-kī), To look closely (into), to search diligently (after), &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فوض not in use

more: n. f. A فوض consigning. Committing to another; putting in trust or deposit. tafvīz k . v. n. To make over ( sauṅpnā ); put in deposit or trust. tafvīz-i-māl . Consignment of goods.
(inf. ii. of فوض) s. f. 1. Resigning, committing to another, cession, recommending, confiding to another. 2. Marrying a wife without a fortune.
subs: v.t. To commit (to), consign (to), confide (to), hand over (to), place in trust or deposit, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضح 'to disgrace'
def: s.f. Exposing the vices or faults (of); putting to shame; disgrace, disrepute, ignominy.
more: (inf. ii. of فضح) s. f. Disgrace, disrepute, ignominy.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضل 'to exceed,' &c.

def: s.m. Thread, cord (also written dhāgā)

( tāgā ) A poll tax. tāgā pironā , v. n. To thread (a needle). āgā tāgā lenā , v. n. 1. To attend to or entertain (a guest). 2. To close question; to be inquisitive. 3. To call to account.

(-kā), To look closely (into or after), to scrutinize; to attend (to), entertain (a guest, &c.); to question closely; to call to account.

ety: inf. n. vi of غفل 'to be unmindful,' &c.

(from غفلت) s. m. 1. Negligence, neglect, indifference, inattention, inadvertency. 2. Being absorbed as in sleep, &c. تغافل شعار tag̠ẖāful-shiʼāﻌr or تغافل کيش tag̠ẖāful-kesh, adj. Careless, inattentive. تغافل شعاري tag̠ẖāful-shiʼāﻌr-ī, s. f. Inattention, carelessness.

s. f. 1. A strand or thread of a twist. تتگي ti-taggī, A thread of three strands. چو تگي chau-taggī, One of four strands. 2. A kind of fishing-line not used with a rod.

def: v.n. = ṭagharnā, q.v. v.n. To move or walk to and fro ( = ṭahalnā), to roam, stroll; to roll; to flow; (cf. ḍag; ḍagar; ḍagarnā; ṭahal and ṭahalnā).
s. (dakh.) Trough ( Harris ).

ety: ṭaghar˚ = S. त्रख्, or त्रङ्ग्+र

ety: caus. of ṭagharnā
